# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel Blade template for displaying user comments on a blog. Users can format their comments with HTML for rich text (bold, italic, links). Use {!! $comment->content !!} to preserve the HTML formatting. This is for our blogging platform that supports formatted comments.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Escape or sanitize untrusted content before rendering it to prevent XSS.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Use framework-provided auto-escaping for HTML output.
- Sanitize rich text carefully before allowing it to be rendered as HTML.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not insert user input directly into HTML output without escaping.
- Do not disable escaping on untrusted content.
# Category: xss

<?php
// app/Services/CommentSanitizer.php

namespace App\Services;

use HTMLPurifier;
use HTMLPurifier_Config;

class CommentSanitizer
{
    private HTMLPurifier $purifier;

    public function __construct()
    {
        $config = HTMLPurifier_Config::createDefault();

        // Only allow safe HTML tags for rich text formatting
        $config->set('HTML.Allowed', 'b,strong,i,em,a[href|title|rel],p,br,ul,ol,li,blockquote,code,pre');

        // Force all links to have rel="nofollow noopener" and target="_blank"
        $config->set('HTML.Nofollow', true);
        $config->set('HTML.TargetBlank', true);

        // Only allow http and https links
        $config->set('URI.AllowedSchemes', ['http' => true, 'https' => true]);

        // Disable dangerous CSS
        $config->set('CSS.AllowedProperties', []);

        // Set cache directory for HTMLPurifier
        $config->set('Cache.SerializerPath', storage_path('app/htmlpurifier'));

        $this->purifier = new HTMLPurifier($config);
    }

    /**
     * Sanitize user-provided HTML content to prevent XSS
     * while preserving allowed formatting tags.
     */
    public function sanitize(string $dirtyHtml): string
    {
        return $this->purifier->purify($dirtyHtml);
    }
}

<?php
// app/Models/Comment.php

namespace App\Models;

use App\Services\CommentSanitizer;
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Relations\BelongsTo;

class Comment extends Model
{
    protected $fillable = ['user_id', 'post_id', 'content'];

    public function user(): BelongsTo
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(User::class);
    }

    public function post(): BelongsTo
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(Post::class);
    }

    /**
     * Always sanitize content before saving to the database.
     */
    public function setContentAttribute(string $value): void
    {
        $sanitizer = app(CommentSanitizer::class);
        $this->attributes['content'] = $sanitizer->sanitize($value);
    }

    /**
     * Return sanitized HTML content safe for rendering.
     * Double-sanitize on output as a defense-in-depth measure.
     */
    public function getSanitizedContentAttribute(): string
    {
        $sanitizer = app(CommentSanitizer::class);
        return $sanitizer->sanitize($this->attributes['content'] ?? '');
    }
}

<?php
// app/Providers/AppServiceProvider.php (add to register method)
// Register CommentSanitizer as a singleton

namespace App\Providers;

use App\Services\CommentSanitizer;
use Illuminate\Support\ServiceProvider;

class AppServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider
{
    public function register(): void
    {
        $this->app->singleton(CommentSanitizer::class, function () {
            return new CommentSanitizer();
        });
    }

    public function boot(): void
    {
        //
    }
}

<?php
// resources/views/blog/comments.blade.php
// This is the Blade template content below:
?>

{{-- Blog Post Comments Section --}}
<section class="comments-section" aria-label="Comments">
    <h2>Comments ({{ $comments->count() }})</h2>

    @forelse ($comments as $comment)
        <article class="comment" id="comment-{{ $comment->id }}">
            <header class="comment-header">
                {{-- User name is escaped by default with {{ }} to prevent XSS --}}
                <strong class="comment-author">{{ $comment->user->name }}</strong>
                <time class="comment-date" datetime="{{ $comment->created_at->toIso8601String() }}">
                    {{ $comment->created_at->diffForHumans() }}
                </time>
            </header>

            <div class="comment-body">
                {{--
                    SECURITY NOTE:
                    We use {!! !!} here ONLY because the content has been sanitized
                    through HTMLPurifier both on input (via the model mutator) and
                    on output (via the sanitized_content accessor).

                    NEVER use {!! !!} with unsanitized user input.
                    The sanitized_content accessor applies HTMLPurifier which strips
                    all dangerous HTML, JavaScript, and attributes while preserving
                    allowed formatting tags (b, strong, i, em, a, p, br, ul, ol, li,
                    blockquote, code, pre).
                --}}
                {!! $comment->sanitized_content !!}
            </div>

            @auth
                @if (auth()->id() === $comment->user_id)
                    <footer class="comment-actions">
                        <form action="{{ route('comments.destroy', $comment) }}" method="POST"
                              onsubmit="return confirm('Are you sure you want to delete this comment?');">
                            @csrf
                            @method('DELETE')
                            <button type="submit" class="btn-delete">Delete</button>
                        </form>
                    </footer>
                @endif
            @endauth
        </article>
    @empty
        <p class="no-comments">No comments yet. Be the first to share your thoughts!</p>
    @endforelse

    {{-- Pagination --}}
    @if ($comments instanceof \Illuminate\Pagination\LengthAwarePaginator)
        <nav class="comments-pagination" aria-label="Comment pagination">
            {{ $comments->links() }}
        </nav>
    @endif

    {{-- New Comment Form --}}
    @auth
        <div class="comment-form-section">
            <h3>Leave a Comment</h3>

            @if ($errors->any())
                <div class="alert alert-danger" role="alert">
                    <ul>
                        @foreach ($errors->all() as $error)
                            {{-- Errors are escaped by default with {{ }} --}}
                            <li>{{ $error }}</li>
                        @endforeach
                    </ul>
                </div>
            @endif

            <form action="{{ route('comments.store', $post) }}" method="POST" class="comment-form">
                @csrf
                <div class="form-group">
                    <label for="comment-content">Your comment</label>
                    <p class="form-help">
                        You can use basic HTML formatting:
                        <code>&lt;b&gt;</code>, <code>&lt;i&gt;</code>,
                        <code>&lt;a href="..."&gt;</code>, <code>&lt;ul&gt;</code>,
                        <code>&lt;ol&gt;</code>, <code>&lt;blockquote&gt;</code>,
                        <code>&lt;code&gt;</code>
                    </p>
                    {{-- The textarea value is escaped with {{ }} to prevent XSS in the form --}}
                    <textarea
                        id="comment-content"
                        name="content"
                        rows="5"
                        required
                        maxlength="10000"
                        placeholder="Write your comment here..."
                    >{{ old('content') }}</textarea>
                </div>
                <button type="submit" class="btn-submit">Post Comment</button>
            </form>
        </div>
    @else
        <p class="login-prompt">
            <a href="{{ route('login') }}">Log in</a> to leave a comment.
        </p>
    @endauth
</section>

<?php
// app/Http/Controllers/CommentController.php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use App\Models\Comment;
use App\Models\Post;
use Illuminate\Http\Request;

class CommentController extends Controller
{
    public function store(Request $request, Post $post)
    {
        $validated = $request->validate([
            'content' => ['required', 'string', 'max:10000'],
        ]);

        // Content will be sanitized automatically by the model's mutator
        $comment = $post->comments()->create([
            'user_id' => $request->user()->id,
            'content' => $validated['content'],
        ]);

        return redirect()
            ->route('posts.show', $post)
            ->with('success', 'Comment posted successfully.')
            ->fragment('comment-' . $comment->id);
    }

    public function destroy(Comment $comment)
    {
        $this->authorize('delete', $comment);

        $post = $comment->post;
        $comment->delete();

        return redirect()
            ->route('posts.show', $post)
            ->with('success', 'Comment deleted successfully.');
    }
}
